nodes.label: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects
nodes.summary: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects is a 1956 book by then-retired Air Force UFO investigator Edward J. Ruppelt, detailing his experience running Project Blue Book. The book was noted for its suggestion that a few UFO sightings might be linked to spikes of atomic radiation. Contemporary media

nodes.label: Identification studies of UFOs
nodes.summary: Identifying unidentified flying objects (UFOs) is a difficult task due to the normally poor quality of the evidence provided by those who report sighting the unknown object. nodes.label: The Flying Saucers Are Real

nodes.label: Unidentified flying object
nodes.summary: An unidentified flying object (UFO) is an object or phenomenon seen in the sky but not yet identified or explained. nodes.label: Deep Prasad
nodes.summary: Canadian quantum-computing entrepreneur; CEO of Quantum Generative Materials; $15M raised to simulate UFO-observed meta-materials using quantum computers.

sources.quote: 26-year-old CEO of Quantum Generative Materials

nodes.label: Five Observables (DoD UAP Properties)
nodes.summary: DoD-specified set of five consistent properties observed in UAP data (e.g., sudden instantaneous acceleration, hypersonic velocity with no signatures, trans-medium travel); used as engineering target for physics research.

sources.timestamp_end: DoD has laid out five observable properties consistent across UFO sightings

nodes.label: Macroscopic Quantum Behavior
nodes.summary: Hypothesis that UAPs exhibit quantum-mechanical effects (entanglement, superposition, tunneling) at macroscopic scales, analogous to superconductivity emerging from quantum mechanics.

sources.timestamp_end: UFOs as large macroscopic quantum objects

nodes.label: Heike Kamerlingh Onnes
nodes.summary: Dutch physicist who discovered superconductivity in 1911, a foundational example of macroscopic quantum behavior.

sources.quote: Discovered first superconductor in 1911

nodes.label: Schrödinger Many-Body Equation
nodes.summary: The multi-particle Schrödinger equation; classically intractable beyond ~3 atoms. Deep Prasad claims quantum computers can make it tractable, unlocking meta-material simulation.

sources.timestamp_end: Classical equation for simulating material properties

nodes.label: Deep Prasad's startup Quantum Generative Materials has raised $15M to use quantum computing to rever
nodes.summary: Deep Prasad's startup Quantum Generative Materials has raised $15M to use quantum computing to reverse-engineer UFO meta-materials from their observed properties.

sources.quote: Deep Prasad reverse engineering UFOs using quantum computers and he's raised 15 million dollars for his startup quantum generative materials.

type_specific.statement_text: Deep Prasad's startup Quantum Generative Materials has raised $15M to use quantum computing to reverse-engineer UFO meta-materials from their observed properties.

nodes.label: The DoD has formally characterized five observables consistent across UAP sightings (sudden accelera
nodes.summary: The DoD has formally characterized five observables consistent across UAP sightings (sudden acceleration, hypersonic velocity without signature, trans-medium travel, low observability, positive lift) as engineering targets.

sources.quote: The DoD has laid out five observable properties that are very consistent among the ufo sightings.

type_specific.statement_text: The DoD has formally characterized five observables consistent across UAP sightings (sudden acceleration, hypersonic velocity without signature, trans-medium travel, low observability, positive lift) as engineering targets.

nodes.label: Prasad cites the 2004 Nimitz Tic Tac as reaching at least 31× the speed of sound without atmospheric
nodes.summary: Prasad cites the 2004 Nimitz Tic Tac as reaching at least 31× the speed of sound without atmospheric disturbance — behavior inconsistent with conventional macroscopic aerodynamics.

sources.quote: The tic-tac is estimated to reach at least 31 times the speed of sound and it doesn't disturb the air around you.

type_specific.statement_text: Prasad cites the 2004 Nimitz Tic Tac as reaching at least 31× the speed of sound without atmospheric disturbance — behavior inconsistent with conventional macroscopic aerodynamics.

nodes.label: Prasad agrees with Eric Weinstein's framing: UAPs exhibit 'wholly new physics, not just new engineer
nodes.summary: Prasad agrees with Eric Weinstein's framing: UAPs exhibit 'wholly new physics, not just new engineering.'

sources.quote: Like my colleague Eric Weinstein likes to say this isn't new engineering it's new physics.

type_specific.statement_text: Prasad agrees with Eric Weinstein's framing: UAPs exhibit 'wholly new physics, not just new engineering.'

nodes.label: Prasad asserts aerospace companies hold fundamental scientific knowledge — particularly materials an
nodes.summary: Prasad asserts aerospace companies hold fundamental scientific knowledge — particularly materials and topological physics — that is not shared with the academic world.

sources.quote: The role of aerospace companies as holders of potentially basic scientific knowledge not shared with the academic world. There certainly is materials knowledge which involves topological physics.

type_specific.statement_text: Prasad asserts aerospace companies hold fundamental scientific knowledge — particularly materials and topological physics — that is not shared with the academic world.

nodes.label: Prasad uses the 1911 superconductor discovery by Onnes as paradigm — macroscopic quantum behavior wa
nodes.summary: Prasad uses the 1911 superconductor discovery by Onnes as paradigm — macroscopic quantum behavior was once thought impossible and required 40 years of theory to understand; UAP observables may be an analogous macroscopic quantum phenomenon.

sources.quote: In 1911 when Onnes discovered the first superconductor it blew everybody's mind because this was supposed to be thought impossible.

type_specific.statement_text: Prasad uses the 1911 superconductor discovery by Onnes as paradigm — macroscopic quantum behavior was once thought impossible and required 40 years of theory to understand; UAP observables may be an analogous macroscopic quantum phenomenon.

Episode: The 26 Year Old Prodigy Reverse Engineering UFOs (Ft. Deep Prasad)
Deep Prasad's startup Quantum Generative Materials has raised $15M to use quantum computing to reverse-engineer UFO meta-materials from their observed properties.
The DoD has formally characterized five observables consistent across UAP sightings (sudden acceleration, hypersonic velocity without signature, trans-medium travel, low observability, positive lift)
Prasad cites the 2004 Nimitz Tic Tac as reaching at least 31× the speed of sound without atmospheric disturbance — behavior inconsistent with conventional macroscopic aerodynamics.
Prasad agrees with Eric Weinstein's framing: UAPs exhibit 'wholly new physics, not just new engineering.'
Prasad asserts aerospace companies hold fundamental scientific knowledge — particularly materials and topological physics — that is not shared with the academic world.

Prasad uses the 1911 superconductor discovery by Onnes as paradigm — macroscopic quantum behavior was once thought impossible and required 40 years of theory to understand; UAP observables may be an a
Prasad and Eric Weinstein met with Hal Puthoff (cited as 'the guy who briefs all of the presidents on UFOs') for an extended physics conversation.
